docs.title: Architecture Guardrails Library
docs.text:
This library contains the approved architecture guardrails for the organisation. Guardrails are standards, policies, patterns, and rules that guide how systems are built and integrated. Browse by category, search by keyword, or filter by tag.

51 guardrails across 5 categories — 51 approved, 0 under review. Last updated: 2026-04-21.

docs.title: All of Government Cloud Sourcing Strategy
docs.text:
Status: ✅ Approved    Version: 1.0

Field Value ID GUARD-CLOUD-002 Category Cloud Subcategory procurement and vendor management Type policy Owner Chief Digital Officer / Digital Government Team Approved by — Approved date — Review due —

docs.title: Description
docs.text: New Zealand government strategy for procuring secure and resilient cloud services across agencies. Establishes a consolidated Marketplace channel for IaaS and cloud services, introduces security tiering, supports multi-cloud and hybrid approaches, and incorporates Māori data sovereignty and Te Tiriti considerations into cloud procurement.

docs.text:

docs.title: Digital Strategy 2.0 – National Digital Development Framework
docs.text:
Status: ✅ Approved    Version: 1.0

Field Value ID GUARD-GOV-001 Category Governance Subcategory digital-transformation-strategy Type document Owner Chief Digital Officer or Digital Strategy Office Approved by — Approved date — Review due —

docs.title: Description
docs.text: National-level digital strategy document from New Zealand's Ministry of Economic Development outlining the vision and framework for digital development across economy, environment, and society. Covers four pillars: connection, capability, confidence, and content, with emphasis on broadband infrastructure, digital skills, cybersecurity, and inclusive participation.

docs.title: Source document
docs.text:
Digital Strategy 2.0.pdf

Last modified: 2026-04-02

docs.title: Description
docs.text: New Zealand government standard defining 10 core principles and 6 operational protocols for producing official Tier 1 statistics. Covers relevance, integrity, quality, coherence, accessibility, efficiency, privacy protection, respondent management, data frameworks, and statistical records management.

docs.text:
Status: ✅ Approved    Version: 1.0

Field Value ID GUARD-GOV-004 Category Governance Subcategory data governance Type policy Owner Data Governance / Statistics Office Approved by — Approved date — Review due —

docs.title: Description
docs.text: UN Economic Commission for Europe resolution establishing 10 core principles for official statistical agencies, covering professional independence, data confidentiality, scientific standards, and international cooperation. Provides foundational governance framework for statistical data collection, processing, and dissemination in democratic societies.

docs.text:
Status: ✅ Approved    Version: 1.0

Field Value ID GUARD-GOV-006 Category Governance Subcategory data-governance-legislation Type document Owner Data Governance / Statistics New Zealand Approved by — Approved date — Review due —

docs.title: Description
docs.text: New Zealand legislation establishing the framework for collection, management, and use of official statistics and research data across government. Defines roles of Statistics New Zealand and the Government Statistician, sets requirements for data confidentiality and privacy protection, and establishes compliance and enforcement mechanisms.

docs.title: Data Protection and Use Policy (DPUP) v1.2
docs.text:
Status: ✅ Approved    Version: 1.0

Field Value ID GUARD-GOV-007 Category Governance Subcategory data-protection Type policy Owner Data Governance or Privacy Team Approved by — Approved date — Review due —

docs.title: Description
docs.text: A comprehensive policy framework for the respectful, trusted, and transparent use of personal information in the social sector. Establishes five principles (He Tāngata, Manaakitanga, Mana Whakahaere, Kaitiakitanga, Mahitahitanga) and four guidelines (Purpose Matters, Transparency and Choice, Access to Information, Sharing Value) to guide agencies in data collection, use, and sharing practices.

docs.title: Source document
docs.text:
Data-Protection-and-Use-Policy-DPUP-January-2022-Version-1.2.pdf

Last modified: 2026-04-02

docs.title: NZGOAL Software Extension: Open Source Licensing Framework for Government Software
docs.text:
Status: ✅ Approved    Version: 1.0

Field Value ID GUARD-GOV-010 Category Governance Subcategory open-source-policy Type policy Owner Architecture Governance / Policy & Standards Approved by — Approved date — Review due —

docs.title: Description
docs.text: New Zealand Government policy framework for open access and licensing of government-owned software under free and open source software (FOSS) licenses. Provides legal context, policy principles, guidance on selecting appropriate FOSS licenses (permissive vs. copyleft), and a structured review and release process to ensure legally robust public software releases.

docs.text:
Status: ✅ Approved    Version: 1.0

Field Value ID GUARD-GOV-012 Category Governance Subcategory data governance Type policy Owner Data Governance Office Approved by — Approved date — Review due —

docs.title: Description
docs.text: New Zealand government data and information management principles established in 2011, covering seven core principles: open, protected, readily available, trusted and authoritative, well managed, reasonably priced, and reusable. Defines how government agencies should manage data as a public asset with emphasis on accessibility, quality, stewardship, and lifecycle management.

docs.title: NZ Government Web Usability Standard 1.4

docs.text: New Zealand government standard that defines controls for binding entities to their entity information to prevent identity theft and account takeover. Establishes binding assurance levels (1-4) with corresponding factor requirements and ongoing integrity maintenance controls.

It addresses risk management, assurance levels, privacy preservation, and secure credential presentation to reduce identity theft, entitlement fraud, and misrepresentation.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Outlines the New Zealand government's mandatory framework for public cloud service adoption, including information classification, risk assessment requirements, and compliance with the Privacy Act 2020 and Information Security Manual. Government organizations must conduct due diligence using the Risk Discovery Tool covering data sovereignty, security, privacy, and incident response.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: New Zealand government standard establishing information management controls to ensure collected entity information is accurate, distinctive, and suitable for eligibility and capability decisions. Provides four-level assurance framework (IA1–IA4) for risk-based verification of identity information, covering evidence quality, storage, accuracy, and counter-fraud measures.
